comment.body: I think you're confusing almas up/down pivots with intra day supports/resistance. Her pivots are are derived from math, and are directed to possible outcomes from other factors. Theres no guarantees intra day(orders are changing) not every setup plays out.
comment.raw_body: I think you're confusing almas up/down pivots with intra day supports/resistance. Her pivots are are derived from math, and are directed to possible outcomes from other factors. Theres no guarantees intra day(orders are changing) not every setup plays out.

comment.body:
Firstly, I think you’re defining “pivot” incorrectly here. Pivot in this sense is more of a “gateway” then a point to men’s revert. That’s something to watch for too but it’s more like it opens the gate for downside/upside movement. 

These levels she defines are from pre market positioning. Majority of firms positioning themselves before the day even starts, retail and MMs are the main trading volume during the day. If orders hit the tape, yes that can impact the levels and interactions of algos, but it doesn’t happen super often. An example would be captain condor (David Cho I think on X) taking off his positions throughout the day. That will 1000% have an impact on market dynamics throughout the day. Again, there’s a lot more that goes into this than just look at her levels and trade blindly. 

Think of it as a prescription for the day (to quote volsignals). Alma gives scenarios and prescriptions but these can change intraday as more data is presented (macro news, big tape shifts, etc). 

For the pin thesis, upside was clearly referring to power hour (being the last hour of the day). The intraday movement to the downside was a clear signal that was not likely to happen. Downside was invalidated when price bounced and started to stall and move back above 6630/40.

comment.body: I respect Linda and her analysis. There is technically a head and shoulders pattern forming on the Spy. As with Alma's analysis or general TA analysis it is always if this , then type. So tomorrow if 667 is breached it would invalidate the H&S pattern however if 667 is respected along with breaching through neckline at 660  this might be a good fall ( 6%). This is assuming no tape bomb manipulation.

comment.body:
Yup, if that happened I would’ve said the same thing. The reason being is it retested vanna flip as resistance and rejected. Again, this is a game of scenarios and price action is always king among many other things from my point of view. 

You definitely can call both scenarios. This game is not a game of certainty, it’s probabilities. Your demeanor and responses are asking for a sure thing, which doesn’t exist in life much less in markets. You are guaranteed nothing, and something to live by that was coined by Mark Douglas (trading in the zone) is all it takes is 1 trader to make a trade to invalidate your edge. We’re working edges here, an edge is a probability that is randomly distributed into your favor. Ponder on that, I’ll let Alma say what she wants if she has anything else to say
